description: A minimalist designed graphic styled as a clean news-article headline card on a plain white background. A small blue all-caps kicker reads 'NEWS' in the upper-left, beneath which is a large bold black serif headline: 'Vocational boom: Enrollment surges at career technical schools as students seek skills, jobs, financial stability.' There is no photograph, illustration, or agency seal visible in the cropped frame; the design relies entirely on typography to present a press headline endorsing the administration's trade-school/workforce narrative. This is a recurring DOL format of surfacing favorable media headlines as social-media tiles. Propaganda technique: borrowing the authority/objectivity of a news outlet's headline to validate the claim (in the tweet) that 'Trade Schools are BOOMING' thanks to the President's Pell Grant expansion. Target audience: students considering trades, parents, and supporters of the administration's labor agenda.
notable_text: NEWS - Vocational boom: Enrollment surges at career technical schools as students seek skills, jobs, financial stability
